name: Cundinamarca (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the sky over the Cundinamarcan altiplano and the Bogota savannah
the gold and natural wealth of the territory
the blood shed by the patriots of Cundinamarca during the wars of independence

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of light blue, yellow, and red, with the coat of arms of Cundinamarca placed towards the hoist.
history: Cundinamarca takes its name from the State of Cundinamarca proclaimed in 1811, one of the first revolutionary states in Spanish America. The flag's colours and arms preserve that early-republican heritage. The department surrounds the Capital District of Bogota.

features.symbolism: the colours of the historic State of Cundinamarca during the early Republic, recalling its role as the cradle of independence
the department's coat of arms; Cundinamarca was one of the first sovereign states declared in 1811 and its arms recall that revolutionary heritage
